def solve(grid)
    m = grid.length
    n = grid[0].length
    max_xor = 1024 # Since grid elements are <= 1023

    # DP[i][j] will be an array of size 1024, where DP[i][j][k] is true 
    # if XOR sum k is reachable at cell (i, j).
    dp = Array.new(m) { Array.new(n) { Array.new(max_xor, false) } }

    # Base case (0, 0)
    g00 = grid[0][0]
    dp[0][0][g00] = true

    # Fill first row (i = 0)
    for j in 1...n
        gj = grid[0][j]
        # Copy reachable XOR sums from (0, j-1) and XOR with grid[0][j]
        for k in 0...max_xor
            if dp[0][j-1][k]
                new_k = k ^ gj
                dp[0][j][new_k] = true
            end
        end
    end

    # Fill first column (j = 0)
    for i in 1...m
        gi0 = grid[i][0]
        # Copy reachable XOR sums from (i-1, 0) and XOR with grid[i][0]
        for k in 0...max_xor
            if dp[i-1][0][k]
                new_k = k ^ gi0
                dp[i][0][new_k] = true
            end
        end
    end

    # Fill the rest of the grid
    for i in 1...m
        for j in 1...n
            gij = grid[i][j]
            
            # From above (i-1, j)
            for k in 0...max_xor
                if dp[i-1][j][k]
                    new_k = k ^ gij
                    dp[i][j][new_k] = true
                end
            end

            # From left (i, j-1)
            for k in 0...max_xor
                if dp[i][j-1][k]
                    new_k = k ^ gij
                    dp[i][j][new_k] = true
                end
            end
        end
    end

    # Find the minimum reachable XOR sum at (m-1, n-1)
    target_row = m - 1
    target_col = n - 1
    
    for k in 0...max_xor
        if dp[target_row][target_col][k]
            return k
        end
    end
    
    # Should theoretically never be reached given the constraints
    return -1
end
